# Phase 10 Runtime Governance Stabilization Plan

## Objective

Stabilize final pre-operational runtime governance boundaries without enabling runtime or creating execution authority.

## Scope

- Runtime governance stabilization doctrine.
- Final non-execution containment.
- Pre-operational constitutional readiness.
- Runtime restraint standards.
- Federated runtime stabilization.
- Adapter governance stabilization.
- Anti-runtime-drift safeguards.
- Operational survivability readiness.
- Execution restraint philosophy.
- Pre-runtime coherence verification.
- Runtime boundary permanence.
- Final governance/runtime separation verification.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- APIs.
- Provider onboarding.
- Adapter implementation.
- Credentials.
- OAuth.
- Prisma or Supabase changes.
- Twilio or email execution.
- Background jobs.
- Activation systems.
- Authorization engines.
- Autonomous approval systems.
- Deployment authority.
- Industry-module execution.
- Repository mutation systems.
- Rollback execution.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Runtime remains disabled.
- Governance remains review-only and containment-only.
- Shared core remains protected.
- Industry modules remain scoped and adapter-driven.
- Federated entities remain sovereign.
- Treasury and runtime remain separated.
- Human approval sovereignty remains intact.
- Runtime drift fails closed.
- Boundary ambiguity blocks progression.
